ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Tom Yum chicken slice noodle; mapped to the closest parent class 'noodle dish' (FOODON_00002473) as the most defensible ancestor available in the ontology.
ontology.foodex2_description: Noodle-based dishes — composite cooked dish with noodles as the primary starch component
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Tom Yum chicken noodle; A00LC (composite noodle-based dish) is the closest defensible parent category available.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept maps to this specific hawker noodle dish; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard hawker bowl of T3 Chicken Slice Noodle weighs approximately 400 g, comprising noodles, chicken slices, leafy greens, and Tom Yum broth.

nutrient_source.title: T3 Chicken Slice Noodle — HPB Singapore Foodo nutrient record
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for T3 Chicken Slice Noodle (cr_id G00010406) with confidence 0.95. All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat is not separately reported by HPB for this entry; estimated at approximately 0.4 g/100g based on the known fat composition of skinless chicken breast and light broth (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy reference for chicken breast, cooked, used as a cross-check).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — rice noodles and wheat noodle analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for T3 Chicken Slice Noodle as a composite dish. GI estimated at 52 (low) based on the analogous food 'rice noodles, boiled' (GI ~53, University of Sydney GI Database) and 'wheat noodles, boiled' (GI ~47–52). The broth-based preparation and protein content from chicken slices further moderate the glycemic response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (52) × available carbohydrate per serving (400 g × 6.21 g/100g = 24.84 g) ÷ 100 = 12.9, rounded to 13.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 248 mg/100g; a 400 g serving delivers approximately 992 mg sodium, representing ~43% of the 2,300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced soy sauce and less broth, avoid MSG additions, and limit portion to one bowl to keep sodium intake within safe daily targets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~52) with modest carbohydrate at 6.21 g/100g and sugar at 0.81 g/100g; protein at 3.84 g/100g helps moderate postprandial glucose response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes can include this dish in a balanced meal plan by choosing rice noodles over wheat noodles for a lower GI, controlling portion size to limit total carbohydrate load, and avoiding added sweet sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat at 1.4 g/100g with estimated saturated fat of ~0.4 g/100g and cholesterol at 17 mg/100g; sodium is the primary cardiovascular concern.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is low in saturated fat and cholesterol, making it suitable for cardiovascular health when sodium is managed by requesting reduced soy sauce and limiting broth consumption.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (~0.4 g/100g); leafy greens and Tom Yum aromatics (lemongrass, galangal) provide anti-inflammatory phytonutrients.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis may benefit from this dish's anti-inflammatory ingredient profile, but should request reduced soy sauce to minimise sodium-related tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: T3 Chicken Slice Noodle is a Thai-influenced hawker noodle dish served in Singapore, featuring tender slices of poached or lightly cooked chicken breast in a zesty, mildly spicy Tom Yum broth. The dish is assembled with boiled noodles (wheat or rice), leafy greens such as bok choy, and aromatic Tom Yum broth made from lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, chilli, and fish sauce. The flavour profile is sour, spicy, and savoury with a light, clean finish. The texture combines soft noodles, tender chicken, and crisp-cooked greens in a clear, fragrant broth. Nutritionally, the dish is low in calories at 53 kcal/100g, low in fat at 1.4 g/100g, and provides moderate protein at 3.84 g/100g. Carbohydrate content is modest at 6.21 g/100g with low sugar at 0.81 g/100g, making it suitable for blood glucose management. Sodium at 248 mg/100g is the primary clinical concern, particularly for hypertension and cardiovascular disease patients. The dish has a low estimated glycemic index (~52) due to the noodle base and protein content. It is suitable for halal diets and is dairy-free. Common in Singapore hawker centres and food courts, it is typically consumed at lunch or dinner as a complete one-bowl meal.
common_user_queries: How many calories in chicken slice noodle?
Is Tom Yum chicken noodle good for diabetics?
Sodium content of chicken noodle soup Singapore
Low calorie noodle dish Singapore hawker
T3 chicken noodle GI glycemic index
Is chicken slice noodle suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010406) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The record is marked as reviewed and canonical. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA SR Legacy chicken breast data as HPB does not separately report this sub-fraction. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for rice/wheat noodles.
notes: 1. Saturated fat estimated at 0.4 g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for cooked skinless chicken breast and light broth, as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this composite hawker dish are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 3. GI is an analogous estimate (~52, low) based on rice noodles and wheat noodles from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this composite dish. 4. The dish is described as Thai-influenced but is served in Singapore hawker centres; multilingual names cover both Thai script and Singaporean languages (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) to reflect its dual cultural context. 5. Halal status is assumed based on typical hawker preparation with chicken and fish sauce; kosher status is not applicable. 6. foodon_match_type and foodex2_match_type set to 'closest_parent' as no exact ontology term exists for this specific composite dish; gap notes document the mapping rationale.
